records.ety: [OE. emprenten, F. empreint, p. p. of empreindre to imprint, fr. L. imprimere to impres, imprint. See 1st In-, Print, and cf. Impress.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imptrinted; p. pr. & vb. n. Imprinting.]

defs: To impress; to mark by pressure; to indent; to stamp.
quotes: And sees his num'rous herds imprint her sands.

defs: To stamp or mark, as letters on paper, by means of type, plates, stamps, or the like; to print the mark (figures, letters, etc., upon something).
quotes: Nature imprints upon whate'er we see, That has a heart and life in it, “Be free.”

defs: To fix indelibly or permanently, as in the mind or memory; to impress.
quotes: Ideas of those two different things distinctly imprinted on his mind.

defs: To create or acquire (a behavioral pattern) by the process of imprinting.

records.ety: [Cf. F. empreinte impress, stamp. See Imprint, v. t.]

defs: Whatever is impressed or imprinted; the impress or mark left by something; specifically, the name of the printer or publisher (usually) with the time and place of issue, in the title-page of a book, or on any printed sheet.
